other_observers: Diane Luck, Dave Elwonger; Mark Peterson found this bird about 30 minutes earlier.
description: This was a small gull about the size of a RBGU, but with a shade or two darker mantle. It had an unmarked thin and short yellow bill. One the mantle were both wide whitish scapular and tertial crescents.  The leg color was yellow. The head was densely streaked dark brown. This bird is unique in that the bird's left foot appears broken or bent at an odd angle.Behaviors: Loafing in and out of the water. When out, it was on the ice.Call: non heardPlumage: basic adult
